item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Full Licensing Information:

The image that is used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Source: SCP Foundation Wiki
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: littlebirdhouseinyoursoul.png
Author: faminepulse
Release year: 2013

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image was taken by faminepulse and is CC-compliant.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Another one I got a chance to speak to Roget about with respect to his headcanon regarding this particular anomaly. According to the information gained therein, these signs not only can be found in mundane urban areas, but also anomalous urban areas as well (ie Three Portlands, Backdoor SoHo, and the Chūgoku Cellar. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1559 is a set of 11 17 posters depicting the phrase "A FEW STILL LISTEN TO BIRDS". SCP-1559 manifestations occur primarily in public places, such as office buildings, strip malls, billboards, mailboxes, public phone booths, and parking garages. All SCP-1559 manifestations have occurred in areas considered to be heavily urbanized.

To this:

SCP-1559 is a set of 11 17 posters depicting the phrase "A FEW STILL LISTEN TO BIRDS". SCP-1559 manifestations occur primarily in public places, such as office buildings, strip malls, billboards, mailboxes, public phone booths, and parking garages. All SCP-1559 manifestations have occurred in areas considered to be heavily urbanized1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. In addition to non-anomalous urban regions, SCP-1559 has also been found in Three Portlands, Backdoor SoHo, and the Chūgoku Cellar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, an original idea and thought behind it and just weird enough to merit locking away. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I need to find that story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how it simply presents an idea, takes it to its logical conclusion

This is why I don't like this. It's really, really obvious, and (as I said to another SCP today already) not in a charming or interesting way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The radical arm of Dr. Doolittle strikes again! I thought this was interesting and well thought out. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kind of severely meh, but I had to upvote for using parrots to communicate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
me too, it was a pretty neat miror

On topic: This is nice. Not my favorite ever, but I do quite like it. A nice and neat execution of the main concept without too much extraneous nonsense like in my entries I mean what . +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WUT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actual footage of Aviania Incorporated's bird-related anomaly production line

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ROGET! I HAVE AN IDEA!
So between this, SCP-1387, and SCP-755, we now have at least 3 skips involving weird bird phenomena. My thought is, they could be connected in-universe. Perhaps a GOI, or a tale relating the three. What do you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I won't lie, I'm in love with the title, but the actual article falls flat for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Birdsoul In Your House

gdi roget +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They tried, but researchers couldn't get them to say anything but "nevermore". :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd use ravens personally. But I like this anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… oh, forgot a detail. What I meant by "it might reduce the extent to which this method is useful for communication" —

well, there's a story of a parrot who lived in an office, and learned to imitate default ringtones because it was funny to watch all the humans reach for their cellphones at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, that's not quite what I meant when I suggested parrots. What i meant is, if you teach a parrot to say "Hello", and then it says "Hello" within earshot of the affected subject, the test subject will then be able to perceive the word "Hello" (as long as you say it like the parrot did, "hellOOOOOO" — it's like the difference between the word "chirp" and an actual chirp).

Teach the parrot a few simple phrases - "come here", "eat this", "good girl". If the parrot can then be coaxed into speaking those phrases in front of the subject; from that point on, the subject's brain will consider those to be "bird noises" — and thus, when the subject hears those phrases, they will not be anomalously replaced with bird noises.

Of course, since all non-bird noises are being replaced with bird noises, that might reduce the extent to which this method is useful for communication…

(unless we bring in SCP-1849, of course)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna point out that this IS The SCP Foundation you're talking about here. They could probably train/breed parrots capable of giving lectures to world class universities if it were required.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As time progresses, subjects will be unable to feel any tactile sensation not related to birds. Behaviors such as coating homes or apartments with feathers, seeking out wild birds to listen to, and meditating intently have been reported.

I liked that detail the most about this article, fits perfectly with the whole theme. Otherwise I thought it was pretty good, but yeah, Parrots don't work like that.

Though I suppose if you trained one well enough…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personnel have been able to communicate with affected subjects via the use of a parrot, which imitated sounds produced by researchers.

That's not actually how parrots work. They say what they damn well please. Even Alex, the best nonhuman talker in history, wasn't a perfect little recording machine. They don't understand sentence structure too well either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1559 is a set of 11 17 posters

Very interesting detail. How many more of these lie undiscovered?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah about that i liked the original of that one better still

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's okay Esko, Yoric's version of that flopped. It's the Voct version up now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first and I pray to God last time I will ever say this, but: Yoric did it better. "Cognitohazardous message designed for public display" is a hell of a small niche, and I don't think this quite makes it for me. I'll no vote it because I don't dislike it per se, but it don't work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it simply presents an idea, takes it to its logical conclusion, and has the Foundation dissect that conclusion. And it's a good idea, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image courtesy of Faminepulse

e: Also Voct helped out a lot because he is a good scp helper buddy.
